query: Best family beach near Barcelona with address and transit instructions

result.query: Best family beach near Barcelona with address and transit instructions
result.answer: Platja Mar Bella in Barcelona is located at Passeig Marítim de la Mar Bella, 104, with coordinates 41°25′51.46″N, 2°14′20.51″E.

answer:
**Platja Mar Bella (Barcelona)**
- Location/Transit: Metro L4 (yellow line) to Selva de Mar station, then 5-minute walk. Alternatively, take the metro to Poblenou and walk a few blocks.
- Address: Passeig Marítim de la Mar Bella, 104, 08005 Barcelona
- Hours/Schedule: Open year-round; best visited May-September for swimming
- Price: Free public beach
- Why It Fits: Known for being quieter than Barceloneta, with nearby SkatePark Mar Bella (great for active kids), accessible facilities for disabled visitors, and local atmosphere. The beach has calm waters suitable for children.
- Confidence: HIGH — multiple sources confirm address and metro access

**Platja de Castelldefels (Castelldefels)**
- Location/Transit: R2 Sud train from Barcelona-Sants (direction Castelldefels) — 20 minutes, approximately €4.50 one-way. Walk 10 minutes from station to beach.
- Address: Platja de Castelldefels, Castelldefels (south of Barcelona)
- Hours/Schedule: Best May-September; boardwalk ideal for strollers
- Price: Free public beach
- Why It Fits: Wide sandy beach with boardwalk — perfect for families with children. The train makes it a practical day trip. Described as "the most practical beach near Barcelona" and "underrated."
- Confidence: HIGH — train timing and beach features confirmed

**Platja Nova Icària (Barcelona)**
- Location/Transit: Metro L4 to Ciutadella-Vila Olímpica station, 5-minute walk to beach
- Address: Located between Barceloneta and Bogatell beaches, 08003 Barcelona
- Hours/Schedule: Open year-round; peak season May-September
- Price: Free public beach
- Why It Fits: Specifically recommended as "one of the most family-friendly beaches in Barcelona" with calm water, volleyball courts, and a playground nearby. Quieter than Barceloneta but still has amenities.
- Confidence: MEDIUM — family-friendly reputation confirmed, but specific address details limited

**Sitges**
- Location/Transit: R2 Sud train from Barcelona-Sants to Sitges — 35 minutes, approximately €6-7 one-way. Multiple beaches accessible from the station.
- Address: Various beaches along Sitges waterfront, 08870 Sitges
- Hours/Schedule: Main beaches open year-round; peak season May-October
- Price: Free public beaches; some beach clubs charge fees
- Why It Fits: 17 beaches in the town, known for family-friendly atmosphere, old town nearby with restaurants. Described as "lively without being crazy overcrowded" — good balance for families wanting amenities.
- Confidence: HIGH — train timing and beach count confirmed

**Recommendation**: For a quick city beach day with minimal transit, **Platja Mar Bella** offers the best combination of specific address, metro access, and family-friendly facilities. For a fuller beach-town experience, **Castelldefels** provides the shortest train ride (20 min) with a boardwalk ideal for families.

sources.snippet: You are here: Home / Travel / Which Barcelona Beach is Best for Families? # Which Barcelona Beach is Best for Families? We knew that we wanted to visit a beach (since they’re so easily accessible from the city!), but we didn’t know exactly *which* was the best Barcelona beach for families, and how we should get to the beach once we decided. Now that we’ve gone, I’ve got what we think are the best Barcelona beaches for a kid-friendly visit. ## Best Beaches for Families in Barcelona, Spain. One of the most surprising things we found out during our visit, was that most of Barcelona’s beaches were actually man made – and quite new! The area along the Mediterranean that is now home to some of the most popular city beaches in the world, was previously entirely industrial. Barceloneta is the closest beach to the city. Child playing on Nova Icaria Beach in Barcelona, one of the best family-friendly beaches in the city.

sources.title: Best beaches near Barcelona for every type of traveler | Trainline
sources.snippet: # Discover the finest beaches near Barcelona by train. Barcelona boasts some of the best urban beaches in the world, lining its palm-fringed coastline. Sunbathe in family-friendly Altafulla, sip cocktails on the beach in Sitges, or spot marine life off the coast of Sant Pol de Mar. Exploring the beaches surrounding Barcelona is straight-forward by train. Hop on a train at Barcelona-Sants and you can head south or north along the Catalonian coast; many beach towns are less than an hour from downtown. Here are our favorite beaches near Barcelona. **Read also:** the best of Spain's mainland beaches. Image Location: Sant Sebastià, Sitges, Spain. A small beach perched on the edge of the Old Town, Sant Sebastià is a favorite of the locals. Dive into the crystalline water at Platja de la Riera — a long flat sandy stretch in upscale Caldes d’Estrach. Surf’s up in El Masnou, just 30 minutes north by train from Barcelona.

sources.title: Platja de la Mar Bella — Beach Review | Condé Nast Traveler
sources.snippet: Mar Bella translates as “beautiful sea,” and whether it’s through the power of suggestion—or geography—the sea here does appear to glimmer towards the turquoise. You can’t spot it from the promenade (it’s hidden behind artfully placed rocks), so, as you stroll onto the sand, you may be surprised to discover just how well attended it is—except in terms of garments. Back at street level, SkatePark Mar Bella is one of the city’s best facilities for the skate, scooter, BMX and skateboard scene. And Mar Bella encapsulates that freedom in beach form. 18 Best Beaches in Barcelona to Make the Most of the City Beside the Sea. As the most discerning, up-to-the-minute voice in all things travel, *Condé Nast Traveler* is the global citizen’s bible and muse, offering both inspiration and vital intel.
